result.stdout:
Version:     2.7.0
Purpose:     Automate the exchange of resource files with the Rigi server.

Syntax:      Rigi-CLI.dll <mode> <options>
Arguments:
             <mode>               : upload, download or align.
             <options>            : Task specific options, having pattern '-xyz [<value>]'.


--------
Upload
--------
Usage:   Rigi-CLI.dll upload <options>
Summary: Upload source files to the workspace on the Rigi server, and download resource files with Rigi tokens and XLIFF-files
Returns: 0 if successful, otherwise an error code.
Options:
  -repo <value>
   The folder on the local machine under which the source resource files are located.
  
  -manifest <value>
   There are 2 options to specify the manifest.
   1. Filepath to the manifest file (most commonly used).
      The manifest file includes relative paths to the source resource files, the parser-id, target rule id, and file-id.
      It is used to create a manifest file as part of the package with source files to be uploaded.
   
   2. Specify source files using file patterns
      Format:  "files:<patterns>".
      Example: Select all files that end with '_en.json' and files named 'en-US.resx'.
               "files:*_en.json|en-US.resx"
  
  -tempfolder <value>
   The tool uploads the workspace and manifest in a ZIP file to the Rigi server.
   By default this file is written to the system temp folder before it is uploaded.
   You can override this location by specifying the temp folder.
  
  -clearchecksum
   Clear the checksum information of the previous upload for this project.
   This setting ensures that the source files are uploaded to the workspace.
  
  -nochecksum
   Disable change detection for source and manifest files.
   Checksums will not be used.
  
  -checksumfolder <value>
   Specifies the local path for storing checksums.
   This setting is not recommended!
   When specified, it bypasses storing checksums within the project on the Rigi server.)
  
  -notokens
   Do not download files with Rigi tokens.
   If specified, the -targetfolder option will not be used.
  
  -targetfolder <value>
   By default, the upload method downloads files containing Rigi tokens and extracts them to the -repo folder.
   Use the -targetfolder option to extract files into a specified folder instead of the default -repo folder.
   Use the -notokens option to avoid downloading files containing Rigi tokens.
  
  -workspace <value>
   Specify the subfolder in the workspace where you want to upload files.
   Example: The Rigi project on the Rigi server includes subfolders for different modules (e.g., module1, module2, etc.).
            To upload source files for 'module1', specify the parameter: -workspace "module1".
            This uploads the file 'xyz/en.json' to 'module1/xyz/en.json'.
   If no subfolder is specified, files will be uploaded to the root directory by default.
  
  -xlifffolder <value>
   (Optional) Specifies the folder to extract XLIFF files. If not provided, XLIFF files will not be downloaded.
  
  -tempkeep
   Retain a copy of the ZIP file containing the workspace and manifest that was uploaded to the server.
   This can be useful for debugging purposes.
  
  -locales <value>
   Provide a comma-separated list of locale values to specify the languages or regions for which XLIFF files should be downloaded.
   Do not specify to download target files for all target locales.
   This is used only if -xlifffolder is defined.
  
  -force
   A forced upload will remove files from the workspace if they are not part of the selected files to be uploaded.
  
  -timeout <value>
   The upload starts when the server is idle.
   The operation is cancelled on a timeout (seconds). The default timeout is 2 minutes.
  
  -nosync
   Only upload and assign files to the workspace. Do not synchronize the string list.
  
  -url <value>
   The url to the rigi project
  
  -username <value>
   The username for authentication. It will be ignored if the -token parameter is provided.
  
  -password <value>
   The password for authentication. It will be ignored if the -token parameter is provided.
  
  -token <value>
   The access token for authentication. There are 2 options to specify the token:
   1. -token <value>, where the value is the token 'ey...', or
   2. -token <filepath>, where the filepath is a file that contains the token.
   If this parameter is present, both -username and -password will be ignored.
  

--------
Download
--------
Usage:   Rigi-CLI.dll download <options>
Summary: Download target files from the Rigi server.
Returns: 0 if successful, otherwise an error code.
Options:
  -targetfolder <value>
   The Rigi server returns a ZIP-file with target resource files. The ZIP file will be extracted to this folder.
  
  -workspace <value>
   Generate files for the source files located in the specified subfolder of the workspace.
   Example: The Rigi project on the Rigi server has subfolders for different modules (e.g., module1, module2, etc.).
            To generate files for source files in the 'module1' subfolder,
            and extract them to the -targetfolder without including 'module1' in the path,
            specify the parameter: -workspace "module1".
            This will extract 'module1/de-DE.json' to 'de-DE.json' in the -targetfolder.
   If unspecified, files will be generated for all source files in the workspace.
  
  -locales <value>
   A comma-separated list of locale values that specify the languages or regions for which target files must be downloaded.
   If not defined, then the target files for all locales will be downloaded (unless -nolocales is set).
  
  -nolocales
   No target languages will be downloaded (overrides locales parameter)
  
  -pseudo
   Download the files with Rigi tokens.
  
  -master
   Download the (mastered) source files.
  
  -master-locale
   Download the master as target language.
  
  -mt
   Apply a machine-translation for untranslated strings before dowmnloading..
  
  -writestates <value>
   A comma-separated list with states. Supported parsers will only write strings with the listed states.
   By default all strings are written to the target resource files.
   Possible values: untranslated, translated, reviewed, validated.
  
  -url <value>
   The url to the rigi project
  
  -username <value>
   The username for authentication. It will be ignored if the -token parameter is provided.
  
  -password <value>
   The password for authentication. It will be ignored if the -token parameter is provided.
  
  -token <value>
   The access token for authentication. There are 2 options to specify the token:
   1. -token <value>, where the value is the token 'ey...', or
   2. -token <filepath>, where the filepath is a file that contains the token.
   If this parameter is present, both -username and -password will be ignored.
  

--------
Align
--------
Usage:   Rigi-CLI.dll align <options>
Summary: Upload target files to the Rigi server and align translations.
Returns: 0 if successful, otherwise an error code.
Options:
  -repo <value>
   The folder under which the source resource files are located.
   The relative paths for the files to be uploaded will be relative to this folder.
  
  -targetfiles <value>
   The path to the text file in which each line contains a file path to the file that must be uploaded to the workspace.
   The file path can be a relative file path with respect to this file or an absolute path.
   The files will be added to the workspace but not assigned with a fileID.
  
  -locales <value>
   A comma-separated list of locales (e.g. de-DE, nl-NL) for which an alignment must be performed.
   If this parameters is omitted, all locales will be aligned.
  
  -alignstate <value>
   The state to which the target strings must be set after alignment.
   Possible values: untranslated, translated (default), reviewed, validated.
  
  -replacestates <value>
   A comma-separated list with states. Strings that match one of those states are candidate to be overwritten.
   Possible values: untranslated, translated, reviewed, validated.
  
  -workspace <value>
   Specify the subfolder in the workspace where you want to upload files.
   Example: The Rigi project on the Rigi server includes subfolders for different modules (e.g., module1, module2, etc.).
            To upload source files for 'module1', specify the parameter: -workspace "module1".
            This uploads the file 'xyz/de-DE.json' to 'module1/xyz/de_DE.json'.
   If no subfolder is specified, files will be uploaded to the root directory by default.
  
  -tempfolder <value>
   The tool uploads target files in a ZIP file to the Rigi server.
   By default this file is written to the system temp folder before it is uploaded.
   You can override this location by specifying the temp folder.
  
  -tempkeep
   Keep a copy of the zip-file that was uploaded to the server.
   This may be helpful for debugging purposes.
  
  -timeout <value>
   The upload starts when the server is idle.
   The operation is cancelled on a timeout (seconds). The default timeout is 2 minutes.
  
  -url <value>
   The url to the rigi project
  
  -username <value>
   The username for authentication. It will be ignored if the -token parameter is provided.
  
  -password <value>
   The password for authentication. It will be ignored if the -token parameter is provided.
  
  -token <value>
   The access token for authentication. There are 2 options to specify the token:
   1. -token <value>, where the value is the token 'ey...', or
   2. -token <filepath>, where the filepath is a file that contains the token.
   If this parameter is present, both -username and -password will be ignored.
